function.name: initialize_project
function.description: Create the base GDevelop project. Must be called first before any other editing tool (it is one of only two tools that do not require an existing project). Creates a new project named 'project_name'. If 'template_slug' names a starter example, the project is initialized from that template; if it is empty/'none'/'empty', an empty project with a single scene is created. Optionally returns the events text of all existing scenes when 'also_read_existing_events' is true (useful right after creating from a template).

project_name.description: Name of the new project/game to create.

template_slug.description: Starter template/example slug to initialize from (e.g. 'starting-first-person-shooter'). Use '', 'none', or 'empty' to create an empty project with a single scene.

also_read_existing_events.description: If true, returns the events-as-text for every scene of the created project (helpful when initializing from a template to inspect its logic).

_notes: project_name and template_slug both read via extractRequiredString (throw if missing) => required. also_read_existing_events via SafeExtractor.extractBooleanProperty => optional. template_slug values '', 'none', 'empty' (case-insensitive) map to an empty project. modifiesProject: true.

function.name: get_game_starter_summary
function.description: Review/summarize a starter game template before planning. Handled entirely on the backend (the client launch is a no-op that returns a failure telling you it is backend-handled); used during planning to study a starter template named by 'template_slug'. One of the two tools that do not require an existing project.

template_slug.description: Slug of the starter template to summarize (e.g. 'starting-first-person-shooter').

_notes: template_slug read via SafeExtractor.extractStringProperty (optional, only used for display/humanization). launchFunction is backend-only (returns generic failure 'handled on the backend'). modifiesProject: false. This is the only other tool besides initialize_project with _requiresProject false.

function.name: create_scene
function.description: Create a new scene (layout) named 'scene_name'. If the scene already exists, it is left in place (optionally adding a 'UI' layer and/or marking it as the first scene). Optionally adds a top 'UI' layer, sets the scene background color, and marks it as the first (startup) scene.

scene_name.description: Name of the scene to create (or ensure exists).

include_ui_layer.description: If true, add a separate top 'UI' layer to the scene in addition to the base layer.

background_color.description: Background color as a hex string (e.g. '#aabbcc') or rgb; applied only when creating a new scene.

is_first_scene.description: If true, set this scene as the first (startup) scene of the game.

_notes: scene_name via extractRequiredString => required. include_ui_layer & is_first_scene via extractBooleanProperty (optional); background_color via extractStringProperty (optional, hex or rgb parsed by rgbOrHexToHexNumber). modifiesProject: true.

function.name: delete_scene
function.description: Delete the scene named 'scene_name' from the project. If the scene does not exist, succeeds as a no-op.

scene_name.description: Name of the scene to delete.

_notes: scene_name via extractRequiredString => required. Only argument. modifiesProject: true.

function.name: inspect_scene_properties_layers_effects
function.description: Read a scene's settings: its properties (name, backgroundColor as hex, stopSoundsOnStartup, isFirstScene, plus project-level gameResolutionWidth/Height, gameOrientation, gameScaleMode, gameName), its ordered layers (name + position), and each layer's effects (effectName, effectType, and effect properties). Read-only. Returns a failure if the scene is not found.

scene_name.description: Name of the scene to inspect.

_notes: scene_name via extractRequiredString => required. Only argument. modifiesProject: false (read-only inspector).

function.name: change_scene_properties_layers_effects_groups
function.description: Mutate a scene in one call: change scene/project properties, add/rename/move/delete layers, add/rename/move/delete layer effects (and set effect properties), and create/rename/delete object groups (and set their member objects). All values are passed as STRINGS in {property_name, new_value} items; property names are fuzzy-matched (case/space/underscore/dash-insensitive). Returns a failure if the scene is not found. Each of the four change arrays is optional; provide only the ones you need.

scene_name.description: Name of the scene to modify.

changed_properties.description: Scene/project property changes. Each item is {property_name, new_value} with STRING values. Recognized property_name (fuzzy-matched): 'name' (renames the scene, updating events/references), 'backgroundColor' (hex or rgb), 'stopSoundsOnStartup' ('true'/'false'), 'isFirstScene' ('true' sets this scene as startup), and project-level 'gameResolutionWidth', 'gameResolutionHeight' (integers), 'gameOrientation', 'gameScaleMode', 'gameName'.

property_name.description: Name of the property to change (fuzzy-matched, e.g. 'name', 'backgroundColor', 'stopSoundsOnStartup', 'isFirstScene', 'gameResolutionWidth', 'gameResolutionHeight', 'gameOrientation', 'gameScaleMode', 'gameName').

new_value.description: New value as a string (numbers/booleans/colors are parsed from this string).

changed_layers.description: Layer additions/edits/removals. For each item: if a layer named 'layer_name' does not exist it is created (named 'new_layer_name' if given) at 'new_layer_position' (or appended); if it exists, it can be renamed via 'new_layer_name', moved via 'new_layer_position', or deleted via 'delete_this_layer' (optionally moving its instances to 'move_instances_to_layer').

layer_name.description: Existing layer name to edit, or name to create. Required per item.

new_layer_name.description: New name for the layer (also the name used when creating).

new_layer_position.description: Target position index for the layer.

move_instances_to_layer.description: When deleting, merge/move this layer's instances into the named layer instead of invalidating them.

changed_layer_effects.description: Layer effect additions/edits/removals and effect property changes. Each item targets effect 'effect_name' on layer 'layer_name'. If the effect does not exist and 'effect_type' is given, it is created at 'new_effect_position'. Existing effects can be renamed via 'new_effect_name', moved via 'new_effect_position', or deleted via 'delete_this_effect'. Nested 'changed_properties' (array of {property_name,new_value} STRING values) sets the effect's parameters (parsed to number/boolean/string by the effect metadata type).

layer_name.description: Layer the effect belongs to. Required per item.

effect_name.description: Effect name to create/edit. Required per item.

effect_type.description: Effect type identifier; required to create a new effect.

new_effect_name.description: New name for the effect (also used as the created effect's name).

new_effect_position.description: Target position index for the effect.

changed_properties.description: Effect parameter changes; each {property_name, new_value} with STRING values, coerced to the parameter's type.

changed_groups.description: Object group changes. Each item targets 'group_name' (created if missing). Rename via 'new_group_name', delete via 'delete_this_group', or set members via 'objects' (an array of {object_name}; the group's membership is replaced to exactly match this list).

group_name.description: Group name to create/edit. Required per item.

new_group_name.description: New name for the group (references updated).

objects.description: Desired group membership. The group is updated to contain exactly these objects (others removed, new ones added).

object_name.description: Name of an object to include in the group.

_notes: scene_name via extractRequiredString => required; errors/returns failure if scene not found. changed_properties/changed_layers/changed_layer_effects/changed_groups all via SafeExtractor.extractArrayProperty => optional. Property values are read with extractStringProperty (STRINGS) then coerced: parseInt for resolution width/height, parseFloat for effect 'number' type, newValue.toLowerCase()==='true' for booleans. property_name fuzzy-matched (isFuzzyMatch lowercases and strips spaces/_/-). Per-item required fields: changed_properties item needs property_name+new_value (else skipped with warning); changed_layers needs layer_name; changed_layer_effects needs layer_name and effect_name (effect_type required to CREATE); changed_groups needs group_name; objects items use 'object_name'. modifiesProject: true.

function.name: create_object
function.description: Create a new object in a scene (or as a global object). The object is created either by searching and installing an asset from the GDevelop asset store (provide 'search_terms' and/or 'asset_id', optionally 'description' and 'two_dimensional_view_kind' to guide selection), or as a blank object of an explicit 'object_type' when that type does not need an asset. You can also duplicate an existing object by passing 'duplicated_object_name' (and optionally 'duplicated_object_scene'). 'scene_name' and 'object_name' are required. If an object with 'object_name' already exists it is left unchanged (use create_or_replace_object to replace). Use 'target_object_scope' to choose where the object lives ('scene' or 'global'); setting it to a scope different from an existing object's scope will MOVE the object between scene and global. If 'search_terms'/'asset_id' are omitted and the type has no default asset tag, a blank object is created.

scene_name.description: Name of the scene (layout) the object belongs to / is created in. Must be an existing scene.

object_name.description: Name of the object to create. If it already exists, nothing is changed.

object_type.description: Explicit GDevelop object type (e.g. 'Sprite', 'TextObject::Text', 'PanelSpriteObject::PanelSprite'). If omitted, inferred from an existing object of the same name. If the named object already exists with a different type, the call fails.

target_object_scope.description: Where the object should live. 'scene' = local to scene_name, 'global' = available to all scenes. If it differs from the scope of an already-existing object with the same name, the object is moved between scene and global.

search_terms.description: Free-text search terms used to find a matching asset in the GDevelop asset store.

asset_id.description: Exact or partial asset id to install from the asset store (alternative/complement to search_terms).

description.description: Natural-language description of the desired object, used to help pick the best asset from the store.

two_dimensional_view_kind.description: Hint about the desired 2D viewpoint/style of the asset (e.g. side view, top-down), passed to the asset search.

duplicated_object_name.description: If set, create the new object by duplicating this existing object (copying its type, behaviors, properties and effects) instead of installing an asset.

duplicated_object_scene.description: Scene where 'duplicated_object_name' is found. If omitted, defaults to scene_name (or global objects).

_notes: create_object maps to the same handler (createOrReplaceObject, index.js line 6363) as create_or_replace_object. Required strings via extractRequiredString: scene_name, object_name. All others via SafeExtractor.extractStringProperty/extractBooleanProperty (optional). 'replace_existing_object' (boolean) also read by the handler but is the distinguishing arg for create_or_replace_object; for create_object it is normally omitted/false. target_object_scope switches on 'global' vs 'scene'. If object exists, it is NOT replaced unless replace_existing_object is true.

function.name: create_or_replace_object
function.description: Create an object, or REPLACE the visuals of an existing object with a different asset from the GDevelop asset store while keeping its type/behaviors/properties/effects (asset swap). Set 'replace_existing_object' true to replace an existing object named 'object_name'; replacement requires at least one of 'search_terms', 'description', 'asset_id', or 'two_dimensional_view_kind'. If the object does not exist it is created instead (same behavior as create_object: install an asset via search_terms/asset_id, create a blank object of 'object_type', or duplicate via 'duplicated_object_name'). 'scene_name' and 'object_name' are required. Use 'target_object_scope' ('scene'|'global') to control where the object lives; a differing scope moves the object between scene and global.

scene_name.description: Name of the scene (layout) the object belongs to. Must be an existing scene.

object_name.description: Name of the object to create or replace.

replace_existing_object.description: If true and an object named object_name exists, replace its asset/visuals (keeping type, behaviors, properties, effects). Requires at least one of search_terms/description/asset_id/two_dimensional_view_kind.

object_type.description: Explicit GDevelop object type. If the existing object has a different type, the call fails. Used when creating a new object.

target_object_scope.description: Where the object should live ('scene' or 'global'). A scope differing from an existing object's scope moves it between scene and global.

search_terms.description: Free-text asset-store search terms used to find a replacement/new asset.

asset_id.description: Exact or partial asset id to install from the asset store.

two_dimensional_view_kind.description: Hint about the desired 2D viewpoint/style of the asset.

duplicated_object_name.description: If set (and not replacing), create the new object by duplicating this existing object.

duplicated_object_scene.description: Scene where 'duplicated_object_name' is found. Defaults to scene_name / global objects.

_notes: Handler createOrReplaceObject (index.js line 778, modifiesProject:true). Shared with create_object. Branching: existingObjectShouldBeMoved (scope change) -> move; else replace_existing_object true -> replaceExistingObject (needs search_terms/description/two_dimensional_view_kind/asset_id); else duplicated_object_name -> duplicate; else create. Required via extractRequiredString: scene_name, object_name. All other args optional via SafeExtractor.

function.name: inspect_object_properties
function.description: Read and return the properties of a single object (searched in the given scene first, then global objects). Returns the object's configurable properties (with current values), its behaviors (name + type), and animation names if any. Also reports counts of variables and behaviors with hints to inspect them via inspect_variables / inspect_behavior_properties. Use this before change_object_property to learn the exact property names. Does not modify the project.

scene_name.description: Name of the scene to look in (object is searched in this scene, then in global objects). Must be an existing scene.

object_name.description: Name of the object whose properties to inspect.

_notes: Handler inspectObjectProperties (index.js line 1410, modifiesProject:false). Both args required via extractRequiredString. No optional args.

function.name: change_object_property
function.description: Change one or more configurable properties of an existing object (searched in the given scene, then global objects). Pass 'changed_properties' as an array of {property_name, new_value} entries where BOTH fields are STRINGS (numeric/boolean values must be given as strings). Property names are matched against the object's properties (case/label tolerant). A special property name of 'name' (or 'object_name'/'objectName') renames the object. Call inspect_object_properties first to discover valid property names. Note: instance position/size/rotation are NOT changed here (use the instance tools); resources cannot be added by name only.

scene_name.description: Name of the scene to look in (object searched in this scene, then global objects). Must be an existing scene.

object_name.description: Name of the object to modify.

changed_properties.description: List of property changes to apply. Each item must have 'property_name' and 'new_value', both as strings.

property_name.description: Name of the property to change. Use 'name' (or 'object_name'/'objectName') to rename the object.

new_value.description: New value for the property, as a string (e.g. "true", "42", "Hello").

_notes: Handler changeObjectProperty (index.js line 1549, modifiesProject:true). scene_name & object_name required via extractRequiredString. changed_properties via SafeExtractor.extractArrayProperty (defaults to [] if missing — empty array means no changes). Each item read via SafeExtractor.extractStringProperty(item,'property_name') and ('new_value') — both must be strings (string-typed new_value confirmed). isPropertyForChangingObjectName treats property_name 'name' or 'objectname' (ignoring -,_,space) as a rename.

function.name: describe_instances
function.description: List the instances placed in a scene's layout, across all layers. Returns each instance with a short 'id' (first 10 chars of its persistent UUID — use this id with the instance editing tools), object name, layer, position (x, y, and z for 3D), rotation, and computed width/height/depth (accounting for default size when no custom size is set). Optionally filter to specific objects via 'filter_by_object_name' (a comma-separated list of object names, case-insensitive). Does not modify the project.

scene_name.description: Name of the scene whose instances to describe. Must be an existing scene.

filter_by_object_name.description: Optional comma-separated list of object names to restrict the results to (case-insensitive, whitespace trimmed). Omit to list all instances.

_notes: Handler describeInstances (index.js line 2629, modifiesProject:false). scene_name required via extractRequiredString. filter_by_object_name via SafeExtractor.extractStringProperty, optional, parsed as CSV split on ',' then trimmed/lowercased. Returned instance 'id' = persistentUuid.slice(0,10).

function.name: add_behavior
function.description: Add a behavior (e.g. Platform, PlatformerObject, Physics, Draggable) to an object, or to every object of a group, in a scene. behavior_type is the full behavior type identifier, usually namespaced as 'ExtensionName::BehaviorName' (the extension is auto-installed if needed). behavior_name is an optional instance name; if omitted the behavior's default name is used (recommended, so shared data is shared between objects). Skips/ warns if the behavior already exists, is a default capability, or the object type is incompatible.

scene_name.description: Name of the scene containing the object or group.

object_name.description: Name of the object OR group to add the behavior to. If a group, the behavior is added to every object in it.

behavior_name.description: Optional instance name for the behavior. Omit to use the behavior's default name (recommended).

_notes: Required strings via extractRequiredString: scene_name, object_name, behavior_type. behavior_name optional via SafeExtractor.extractStringProperty. behavior_type containing '::' triggers extension install of the part before '::'.

function.name: remove_behavior
function.description: Remove a behavior (by its instance name) from an object, or from every object of a group, in a scene. Also removes any dependent behaviors that required it. Warns if the named behavior is not present on an object.

scene_name.description: Name of the scene containing the object or group.

object_name.description: Name of the object OR group. If a group, the behavior is removed from every object in it that has it.

behavior_name.description: Instance name of the behavior to remove (not the behavior type).

_notes: All three args required via extractRequiredString. Dependent behaviors are auto-removed too.

function.name: inspect_behavior_properties
function.description: Read the current properties (and shared/behavior-shared-data properties, if any) of a named behavior attached to an object or group in a scene. Returns property names, values, and metadata. Read-only. Use this before change_behavior_property to learn the exact property names and current values.

scene_name.description: Name of the scene containing the object or group.

object_name.description: Name of the object OR group that carries the behavior. For a group, the first object having the behavior is inspected.

behavior_name.description: Instance name of the behavior to inspect.

_notes: All three args required via extractRequiredString. modifiesProject:false. Returns { success, behaviorName, properties, sharedProperties }.

function.name: change_behavior_property
function.description: Change one or more properties of a named behavior on an object or group in a scene. Properties are passed in 'changed_properties' as an array of objects, each with 'property_name' (the exact property name, as seen via inspect_behavior_properties) and 'new_value' (ALWAYS a string, even for numbers/booleans). Matches against both the behavior's own properties and its behavior-shared-data properties. For a group, the change is applied to the behavior on every object that has it. Items missing property_name or new_value are skipped with a warning.

scene_name.description: Name of the scene containing the object or group.

object_name.description: Name of the object OR group carrying the behavior. For a group, applied to all objects that have the behavior.

behavior_name.description: Instance name of the behavior whose properties to change.

changed_properties.description: List of property changes to apply.

new_value.description: New value as a STRING (booleans as 'true'/'false', numbers as their string form).

_notes: scene_name/object_name/behavior_name required via extractRequiredString. changed_properties via SafeExtractor.extractArrayProperty (defaults to [] if absent — but with no changes nothing happens, so effectively required). Each item read via extractStringProperty for 'property_name' and 'new_value' (both strings).

function.name: add_or_edit_variable
function.description: Create, edit, or delete one or more variables in a given scope. variable_scope is 'scene' (requires scene_name), 'object' or 'group' (requires object_name; optionally scene_name — omit scene_name for a global object/group), or 'global'. Provide variables to change in the 'variables' array; each item has 'variable_name_or_path' (a name or dotted/structured path), 'value' (string; required unless deleting), optional 'variable_type' to force the type, and optional 'delete_this_variable' (boolean) to delete instead of set. A single variable may alternatively be passed via top-level variable_name_or_path/value/variable_type without the array.

variable_scope.description: Scope of the variables. 'scene' needs scene_name; 'object'/'group' need object_name (scene_name optional, omit for global object); 'global' needs neither.

scene_name.description: Scene name. Required for variable_scope 'scene'; optional for 'object'/'group' (omit to target a global object/group).

object_name.description: Object or group name. Required for variable_scope 'object' or 'group'.

variables.description: List of variable operations. Preferred way to set/delete one or many variables.

value.description: New value as a string. Required unless delete_this_variable is true.

delete_this_variable.description: If true, delete this variable instead of setting it.

variable_name_or_path.description: Single-variable fallback (used only when 'variables' array is absent).

_notes: variable_scope required via extractRequiredString; scene_name/object_name optional via extractStringProperty (validated at runtime per scope). Operations from 'variables' array (extractArrayProperty); each item: variable_name_or_path/value/variable_type via extractStringProperty (all strings), delete_this_variable via extractBooleanProperty. If 'variables' absent, falls back to top-level variable_name_or_path/value/variable_type. Empty operations -> failure. variable_type enum values inferred (string/number/boolean/structure/array) — code passes it as forcedVariableType.

function.name: inspect_variables
function.description: Read variables in a given scope. variable_scope is 'scene' (needs scene_name), 'object'/'group' (needs object_name, scene_name optional — omit for a global object), or 'global'. Optionally pass 'variable_names_or_paths' (array of strings) to fetch only specific variables/paths; if omitted, returns all variables in the scope. Read-only. Reports any requested paths that were not found.

variable_scope.description: Scope to inspect. 'scene' needs scene_name; 'object'/'group' need object_name (scene_name optional); 'global' needs neither.

scene_name.description: Scene name. Required for 'scene' scope; optional for 'object'/'group'.

object_name.description: Object or group name. Required for 'object' or 'group' scope.

variable_names_or_paths.description: Optional list of specific variable names or paths to read. If omitted, all variables in the scope are returned.

_notes: variable_scope required via extractRequiredString; scene_name/object_name optional via extractStringProperty (validated per scope by resolveVariablesContainers). variable_names_or_paths via extractArrayProperty; non-string entries are dropped. modifiesProject:false. Empty list returns full container.

function.name: put_2d_instances
function.description: Place, move, resize, rotate, or erase 2D instances of an existing object on a layer of a scene, using a virtual 'brush'. Behavior depends on brush_kind: 'point' places/moves all instances at brush_position; 'line' spreads them evenly from brush_position to brush_end_position; 'grid' lays them in a rectangle from brush_position to brush_end_position (column/row counts auto-computed from the rectangle aspect ratio unless row_count/column_count are given); 'random_in_circle' scatters them within brush_size radius of brush_position; 'none' leaves existing instances in place (new ones go to brush_position); 'erase' deletes instances (those listed in existing_instance_ids, and/or instances of object_name within brush_size of brush_position on the given layer). To create new instances, set object_name and new_instances_count. To edit existing instances, pass their ids via existing_instance_ids (get ids from describe_instances, the 'id' field; partial id prefixes match). If brush_position is omitted for placement, instances default to scene center. Positions/sizes are coordinate strings, not arrays.

scene_name.description: Name of the scene (layout) to modify. Required; the call fails if the scene does not exist.

layer_name.description: Target layer name. Use an empty string "" for the base layer. Required; the call fails if a non-empty layer name does not exist in the scene.

brush_kind.description: How instances are positioned/affected. One of: 'point', 'line', 'grid', 'random_in_circle', 'none', 'erase'. 'line' and 'grid' require brush_end_position. Unknown values are treated as 'none'.

object_name.description: Name of the existing object to instantiate. Required when creating new instances (new_instances_count > 0). The object must already exist in the scene or globally. For 'erase' with a brush (no explicit ids), only instances of this object are considered.

brush_position.description: Start/anchor position as two comma-separated numbers 'x,y' (e.g. '100,200'). For 'point' it is the placement position; for 'line'/'grid' it is the start corner; for 'random_in_circle'/'erase' it is the circle center. If omitted for placement, defaults to scene center (gameResolutionWidth/2, gameResolutionHeight/2).

brush_end_position.description: End position as two comma-separated numbers 'x,y'. Required for brush_kind 'line' (end of the line) and 'grid' (opposite corner of the rectangle). Ignored by other brushes.

brush_size.description: Radius in pixels. For 'random_in_circle' it is the scatter radius around brush_position; for 'erase' it is the selection radius (instances within this distance of brush_position are deleted; 0 means exact-position match). Defaults to 0.

existing_instance_ids.description: Comma-separated list of existing instance ids to move/modify/erase (e.g. 'a1b2,c3d4'). Ids come from describe_instances (the 'id' field); each value is matched as a prefix of the instance's persistent UUID. Omit when only creating new instances.

new_instances_count.description: Number of new instances of object_name to create. If 0/omitted and no existing_instance_ids are given, defaults to 1 (or row_count*column_count when both are set for a grid).

instances_size.description: Custom size applied to every affected instance, as two comma-separated numbers 'w,h' (e.g. '64,64'). Sets a custom width/height. Omit to keep the object's default size.

instances_rotation.description: Rotation angle in degrees applied to every affected instance. Omit to leave rotation unchanged.

instances_opacity.description: Opacity applied to every affected instance, 0-255 (0 transparent, 255 opaque). Omit to leave opacity unchanged.

instances_z_order.description: Z-order (draw order) applied to every affected instance. Omit to leave z-order unchanged.

row_count.description: Optional number of rows for the 'grid' brush. If both row_count and column_count are given, they override the auto-computed grid layout (and, when new_instances_count is unset, determine how many instances are created: row_count*column_count).

column_count.description: Optional number of columns for the 'grid' brush. See row_count.

_notes: All extraction confirmed in index.js put2dInstances.launchFunction (lines 2878-3481). Exact arg names via SafeExtractor: scene_name/layer_name/brush_kind via extractRequiredString (required -> call fails if missing). object_name, brush_position, brush_end_position, existing_instance_ids, instances_size via extractStringProperty (optional). brush_size, new_instances_count, instances_z_order, instances_rotation, instances_opacity, row_count, column_count via extractNumberProperty (optional). NOTE: instances_rotation is a NUMBER here (degrees), unlike put_3d_instances where it is a STRING. brush_position and brush_end_position are parsed by parseCommaSeparatedTwoFiniteNumbers => exactly 'x,y'. instances_size parsed by parseCommaSeparatedTwoFiniteNumbers => 'w,h'. existing_instance_ids split on ',' ; each id matched via getPersistentUuid().startsWith(id) (prefix match). Empty layer_name allowed (base layer). brush_kind switch handles 'erase','line','grid','random_in_circle','point', else treated as 'none'. modifiesProject:true set on the function.

function.name: put_3d_instances
function.description: Place, move, resize, rotate, or erase 3D instances of an existing object on a layer of a scene, using a virtual 'brush' in X,Y,Z space. Behavior depends on brush_kind: 'point' places/moves all instances at brush_position; 'line' spreads them evenly from brush_position to brush_end_position in 3D; 'random_in_sphere' scatters them within brush_size radius of brush_position; 'none' leaves existing instances in place (new ones go to brush_position); 'erase' deletes instances (those listed in existing_instance_ids, and/or instances of object_name within brush_size of brush_position on the given layer). To create new instances, set object_name and new_instances_count. To edit existing instances, pass their ids via existing_instance_ids (get ids from describe_instances, the 'id' field; partial id prefixes match). If brush_position is omitted for placement, instances default to (sceneCenterX, sceneCenterY, 0). Positions/sizes/rotations are coordinate strings, not arrays.

scene_name.description: Name of the scene (layout) to modify. Required; the call fails if the scene does not exist.

layer_name.description: Target layer name. Use an empty string "" for the base layer. Required; the call fails if a non-empty layer name does not exist in the scene.

brush_kind.description: How instances are positioned/affected. One of: 'point', 'line', 'random_in_sphere', 'none', 'erase'. 'line' uses brush_end_position. Unknown values are treated as 'none'.

object_name.description: Name of the existing object to instantiate. Required when creating new instances (new_instances_count > 0). The object must already exist in the scene or globally. For 'erase' with a brush (no explicit ids), only instances of this object are considered.

brush_position.description: Start/anchor position as three comma-separated numbers 'x,y,z' (e.g. '100,200,0'). For 'point' it is the placement position; for 'line' the start point; for 'random_in_sphere'/'erase' the sphere center. If omitted for placement, defaults to (gameResolutionWidth/2, gameResolutionHeight/2, 0).

brush_end_position.description: End position as three comma-separated numbers 'x,y,z'. Used by brush_kind 'line' (the end of the 3D line). Ignored by other brushes.

brush_size.description: Radius in 3D units. For 'random_in_sphere' it is the scatter radius around brush_position; for 'erase' it is the selection radius (instances within this 3D distance of brush_position are deleted; <= 0 means exact-position match on x,y,z). Defaults to 0.

existing_instance_ids.description: Comma-separated list of existing instance ids to move/modify/erase (e.g. 'a1b2,c3d4'). Ids come from describe_instances (the 'id' field); each value is matched as a prefix of the instance's persistent UUID. Omit when only creating new instances.

new_instances_count.description: Number of new instances of object_name to create. If 0/omitted and no existing_instance_ids are given, defaults to 1.

instances_size.description: Custom size applied to every affected instance, as three comma-separated numbers 'w,h,d' (width, height, depth, e.g. '64,64,64'). Sets custom width/height/depth. Omit to keep the object's default size.

instances_rotation.description: Rotation applied to every affected instance, as three comma-separated numbers 'rx,ry,rz' in degrees (rotation about X, Y, Z). All three components are required for rotation to apply. Omit to leave rotation unchanged.

_notes: All extraction confirmed in index.js put3dInstances.launchFunction (lines 3490-~3940). Exact arg names via SafeExtractor: scene_name/layer_name/brush_kind via extractRequiredString (required). object_name, brush_position, brush_end_position, existing_instance_ids, instances_size, instances_rotation via extractStringProperty (optional). brush_size, new_instances_count via extractNumberProperty (optional). DIFFERENCES FROM 2D: brush_position/brush_end_position/instances_size use parseCommaSeparatedThreeFiniteNumbers => exactly 'x,y,z' / 'w,h,d' (THREE numbers). instances_rotation is a STRING parsed via split(',').map(parseFloat) and only applied if length>=3 ('rx,ry,rz'). There is NO instances_z_order, instances_opacity, row_count, or column_count for 3D, and NO 'grid'/'random_in_circle' brush; 3D adds 'random_in_sphere'. Default placement Z is 0. instances_size also sets hasCustomDepth + customDepth. existing_instance_ids matched via getPersistentUuid().startsWith(id) (prefix). modifiesProject:true set on the function.

function.name: read_scene_events
function.description: Read and return the full event sheet (events/conditions/actions) of a named scene, rendered as human-readable text. Use this before editing events so you understand existing logic. Errors if the scene does not exist.

scene_name.description: Exact name of the scene (layout) whose events to read. Must match an existing scene.

_notes: launchFunction reads only extractRequiredString(args,'scene_name'). Returns {eventsForSceneNamed, eventsAsText}. Errors via makeGenericFailure if !project.hasLayoutNamed(scene_name).

function.name: add_scene_events
function.description: Generate and insert new events into a scene's event sheet using natural-language descriptions. The IDE invokes a server-side events generator that produces valid GDevelop events from the description(s), installs any required extensions/behaviors/variables, and applies the changes. Provide either a single 'events_description' OR an array of 'event_batches' (each batch describing one chunk of events plus where to place it). Always pass 'extension_names_list'. Requires an active AI request context.

scene_name.description: Exact name of the scene to add events to. Must match an existing scene.

events_description.description: Natural-language description of the events to generate. Provide this OR event_batches. Required if event_batches is not given.

event_batches.description: Alternative to events_description: a list of event chunks to generate, each with its own description and placement info. Must be non-empty and each batch must have a non-empty events_description.

events_description.description: Natural-language description of this batch of events. Required (non-empty) for each batch.

placement_relation.description: How/where to place this batch relative to the target event (e.g. before/after/inside). Defaults to '(unspecified)' if omitted.

placement_target_event_id.description: ID of an existing event to place this batch relative to.

placement_expected_parent_event_id.description: ID of the expected parent event under which this batch should be nested.

placement_rationale.description: Short explanation of why this placement was chosen.

extension_names_list.description: Required. List of GDevelop extension names relevant to generating these events (helps the generator pick correct actions/conditions).

objects_list.description: Optional list of related object names to scope/inform the generation. Defaults to empty string.

placement_hint.description: Optional hint about where/how to place or adapt the generated events.

estimated_complexity.description: Optional estimated complexity of the requested events (numeric hint for the generator).

_notes: launchFunction: extractRequiredString scene_name & extension_names_list (both required, error if missing). extractStringProperty events_description, objects_list (null->''), placement_hint (||''). extractArrayProperty event_batches; per-batch extractStringProperty events_description/placement_relation/placement_target_event_id/placement_expected_parent_event_id/placement_rationale. extractNumberProperty estimated_complexity. Must supply events_description OR non-empty event_batches (each batch needs events_description) else makeGenericFailure. Also requires relatedAiRequestId in context. Calls server-side generateEvents() then applies changes.

function.name: generate_events
function.description: Identical to add_scene_events (same underlying implementation): generate and insert new events into a scene's event sheet from natural-language descriptions. Provide either 'events_description' OR a non-empty 'event_batches' array, plus 'extension_names_list'. The IDE runs a server-side generator that produces valid GDevelop events and applies them.

scene_name.description: Exact name of the scene to add events to. Must match an existing scene.

events_description.description: Natural-language description of the events to generate. Provide this OR event_batches.

event_batches.description: Alternative to events_description: list of event chunks, each with description and placement info. Must be non-empty; each batch must have a non-empty events_description.

events_description.description: Natural-language description of this batch of events. Required for each batch.

placement_relation.description: Placement relation relative to the target event. Defaults to '(unspecified)'.

placement_target_event_id.description: ID of an existing event to place this batch relative to.

placement_expected_parent_event_id.description: ID of the expected parent event for nesting.

extension_names_list.description: Required. List of relevant GDevelop extension names.

objects_list.description: Optional list of related object names. Defaults to empty string.

placement_hint.description: Optional hint about placement/adaptation of generated events.

estimated_complexity.description: Optional numeric complexity estimate for the generator.

_notes: In index.js the registry maps generate_events: addSceneEvents — it is the exact same EditorFunction object/implementation as add_scene_events, so schema/args/required are identical.

function.name: read_game_project_json
function.description: Inspect the overall game project structure. This is a no-op on the client: the IDE sends the function-call output to the backend together with an up-to-date game project JSON, which the backend uses to compute the actual read result. Takes no arguments.

_notes: launchFunction does no SafeExtractor reads and just returns {success:true}; the real result is computed backend-side from the project JSON. No arguments.

function.name: read_full_docs
function.description: Request full GDevelop documentation for one or more extensions. NOTE: the client cannot actually fetch docs — it always returns a failure asking the model to continue with existing GDevelop knowledge.

extension_names.description: Name(s) of the extension(s) to read documentation for.

_notes: launchFunction always returns makeGenericFailure ('Unable to read full documentation...'); it reads no args. Only renderForEditor reads extractStringProperty(args,'extension_names') (optional, not validated/required). So extension_names is the only known arg and is not strictly required by code.

function.name: search_docs
function.description: Search the GDevelop documentation. NOTE: the client cannot actually search — it always returns a failure asking the model to continue with existing GDevelop knowledge.

_notes: Neither launchFunction nor renderForEditor read any args; launchFunction always returns makeGenericFailure. No discoverable argument names in source (likely a query arg conceptually, but the code reads none).
